training_dataset.flag_source: perturbation-labeled synthetic cases (34 value_error + 33 period_error + 33 scale_error) + 44 human-labeled ambiguous FLAG

key_findings: near_tolerance_flag fires on all 16/16 c2b small_value_error cases (rel_err 0.021-0.038 > tolerance 0.005)
V5 catches 16/16 c2b cases as FLAG — V4b caught 0/16; the near-tolerance signal gap is resolved
grounding_confidence_score is #2 SHAP driver for c2b FLAG predictions (0.163) — continuous confidence degrades from 0.863 at 2.1% error to 0.650 at 3.8% error
near_tolerance_flag ranks #10 overall by SHAP (0.001) — binary flag acts as gate, continuous grounding_confidence_score carries discrimination
claim_count leakage detected and removed: TAT-QA answers are bare numbers (count=1) vs synthetic answers include year token (count=2) — format artefact not real signal
REPAIR precision improves from 50% (V4b) to 100% (V5) — GBM learns identity_fail interaction correctly
V5 matches rules on 84-case eval (91.67%) — up from V4b 84.52%; no regression on clear-signal cases
100% intrinsic test accuracy reflects structured synthetic data boundaries, CV min fold 90.4% is honest estimate
V5 wins 17 cases vs rules on intrinsic test set — all are ACCEPT/REPAIR cases that rules over-flag

shap_analysis.method: PermutationExplainer (multiclass-safe; TreeExplainer does not support multiclass GBM)

leakage_incident.cause: TAT-QA ACCEPT answers are bare numbers (claim_count=1); synthetic FLAG answers include year token 'Revenue in YEAR was X.' (claim_count=2). Format artefact perfectly separated ACCEPT from FLAG by source, not by error type.
leakage_incident.detection: 1.0000 train/test accuracy with claim_count present; feature_importance=0.714 for claim_count alone
leakage_incident.resolution: Dropped claim_count from training features. Performance retained (CV 96.9%).

architecture_conclusion: V5 GBM resolves the near-tolerance gap identified in V4b. The combination of near_tolerance_flag (binary gate) and grounding_confidence_score (continuous quality signal) gives the model information to classify financially-material but numerically-within-tolerance errors. GBM's tree-based interactions enable it to learn that (near_tolerance=1 AND grounding_confidence<0.87) implies FLAG while (near_tolerance=0 AND coverage=1.0) implies ACCEPT — a non-linear boundary LR cannot represent with equal compactness.

future_work.real_llm_errors: Collect actual GPT-4/Claude P&L answers for authentic error distribution in training data
future_work.second_annotator: Add second annotator for ambiguous tier to compute inter-annotator agreement (Cohen's kappa)
future_work.answer_format_normalisation: Normalise answer format before signal extraction to prevent future claim_count-style leakage
future_work.grounding_confidence_calibration: Investigate whether grounding_confidence_score thresholding (e.g. <0.75 = suspect) could replace near_tolerance_flag as a single continuous signal
